emergency-twin-spot-lights.intro: Need emergency twin spot lights installed or upgraded to BS 5266 across the North West? faq: Where are emergency twin spot lights used?

Typically in higher-risk or high-ceiling areas such as plant rooms, loading bays, open floors and external escape paths where directional flood-style emergency light is needed beyond standard bulkheads.
Do twin spots need duration testing?
Yes. Like other emergency luminaires they should be function-tested and periodically duration-tested to BS 5266, with results kept in the emergency lighting logbook.
Can you replace failed twin spot batteries or complete units?
Yes. We replace batteries, heads or full twin-spot fittings, then retest and update your records so the system stays compliant.

faq: Do exit signs need to be illuminated in the UK?
Escape-route signage must remain visible in a mains failure; maintained or non-maintained emergency exit signs are specified to suit occupancy, building type and fire risk assessment findings.
Can you install running-man exit signs only?
Yes. We install ISO-style running-man pictograms with correct directional arrows and can phase out outdated text-only legends during upgrades.
How long does exit sign installation take?
A small commercial floor can often be completed in a day; larger multi-storey sites are phased by floor or zone so escape routes stay usable during works.

faq: Are running man exit signs mandatory?
UK practice expects clear, illuminated escape-route signage; running-man pictograms are the widely recognised format and are commonly required by fire risk assessments and insurers.
Can you replace old EXIT text signs with running man legends?
Yes. We swap outdated text signs for compliant LED running-man units and retest the emergency circuit as part of the works.
Do running man signs need battery backup?
Emergency exit signs must remain legible on mains failure for the required duration, so battery-backed emergency versions are used on escape routes.

faq: How often should emergency lighting batteries be replaced?
Replace when duration tests fail or manufacturers’ service life is exceeded—often around four to five years for many self-contained packs, though actual life depends on usage and temperature.
Is a full luminaire replacement better than a battery change?
If the fitting is obsolete, corroded or inefficient, a modern LED emergency unit can be more cost-effective; we advise on site after inspection.
Will you retest after battery replacement?
Yes. We retest and record results so your emergency lighting documentation reflects the work completed.

faq: Is security lighting different from emergency lighting?
Yes. Security lighting is for deterrence and visibility under normal or switched power; emergency lighting is battery-backed for escape routes under BS 5266. We can design both on the same estate.
Can lights be motion-activated only?
PIR-triggered circuits work well for pathways and side elevations; continuous low-level lighting with boost on detection is common for high-risk perimeters.

Yes. We work for freeholders, managing agents, care providers and commercial occupiers across the North West.

faq: What should I do if I smell gas?
Evacuate if necessary, ventilate, avoid electrical switches and flames, and call the National Gas Emergency Service on 0800 111 999. Then contact us for repair once safe.
Can you find small leaks that are hard to smell?
Yes. Instruments and tightness procedures detect pressure loss and help locate joints, valves and appliance connections.
Will you re-light appliances after repair?
After a successful retest we recommission appliances and leave the system safe to use, with advice on any further servicing needed.

Can inspections happen during a tenancy?
